source_paper_title: Lipschitz Stability of Least-Squares Problems Regularized by Functions with C2-Cone Reducible Conjugates
context:
Page 15, Section 3 (discussion immediately after Theorem 3.8)

By fixing the parameter \(b=\bar b\), our condition (45) is also sufficient for the Lipschitz stability of the solution mappings considered after Theorem 3.8. Whether it is necessary is an open question.
title: Necessity of the condition \(\ker \bar A \cap \mathrm{par}\,\partial g^*(\bar z)=\{0\}\) for Lipschitz stability when the data \(b\) is fixed
background_latex:
Let \(X,Y\) be Euclidean spaces and \(g:X\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\) be closed, proper, convex with Fenchel conjugate \(g^*:X\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\). For \(A\in\mathcal{L}(X,Y)\), \(b\in Y\), and \(\mu>0\), consider the regularized least-squares problem
\[
P(A,b,\mu):\quad \min_{x\in X}\; \frac{1}{2\mu}\|Ax-b\|^2 + g(x).
\]
Let the solution mapping be
\[
S(A,b,\mu):=\operatorname*{argmin}_{x\in X}\Big\{\frac{1}{2\mu}\|Ax-b\|^2 + g(x)\Big\}.
\]
Fix \((\bar A,\bar b,\bar\mu)\in\mathcal{L}(X,Y)\times Y\times\mathbb{R}_{++}\) and assume \(\bar x\in S(\bar A,\bar b,\bar\mu)\). Define
\[
\bar z := \frac{1}{\bar\mu}\,\bar A^*(\bar b-\bar A\bar x)\in X.
\]
Assume also that \(g^*\) is \(C^2\)-cone reducible at \(\bar z\). Let \(\partial g^*(\bar z)\) denote the convex subdifferential of \(g^*\) at \(\bar z\), and let \(\mathrm{par}\,\partial g^*(\bar z)\) denote the parallel subspace of the convex set \(\partial g^*(\bar z)\), i.e., \(\mathrm{par}\,\partial g^*(\bar z):=\operatorname{span}(\partial g^*(\bar z)-u)\) for any \(u\in\partial g^*(\bar z)\).
problem_definition_latex:
Prove or disprove the following necessity statement. Suppose that one of the partially-fixed-parameter solution mappings is single-valued and locally Lipschitz around \((\bar A,\bar b,\bar\mu)\), for example:
- \(b\mapsto S(\bar A,b,\bar\mu)\) is single-valued and locally Lipschitz around \(\bar b\), or
- \((A,\mu)\mapsto S(A,\bar b,\mu)\) is single-valued and locally Lipschitz around \((\bar A,\bar\mu)\), or
- \(\mu\mapsto S(\bar A,\bar b,\mu)\) is single-valued and locally Lipschitz around \(\bar\mu\).
Under the standing assumptions above (including \(C^2\)-cone reducibility of \(g^*\) at \(\bar z\)), must the condition
\[
\ker \bar A\;\cap\; \mathrm{par}\,\partial g^*(\bar z)=\{0\}
\]
necessarily hold?
latex:
Let \(X,Y\) be Euclidean spaces and \(g:X\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\) be closed, proper, convex with Fenchel conjugate \(g^*:X\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\). For \(A\in\mathcal{L}(X,Y)\), \(b\in Y\), and \(\mu>0\), consider the regularized least-squares problem
\[
P(A,b,\mu):\quad \min_{x\in X}\; \frac{1}{2\mu}\|Ax-b\|^2 + g(x).
\]
Let the solution mapping be
\[
S(A,b,\mu):=\operatorname*{argmin}_{x\in X}\Big\{\frac{1}{2\mu}\|Ax-b\|^2 + g(x)\Big\}.
\]
Fix \((\bar A,\bar b,\bar\mu)\in\mathcal{L}(X,Y)\times Y\times\mathbb{R}_{++}\) and assume \(\bar x\in S(\bar A,\bar b,\bar\mu)\). Define
\[
\bar z := \frac{1}{\bar\mu}\,\bar A^*(\bar b-\bar A\bar x)\in X.

Let \(\partial g^*(\bar z)\) denote the convex subdifferential of \(g^*\) at \(\bar z\), and let \(\mathrm{par}\,\partial g^*(\bar z)\) denote the parallel subspace of the convex set \(\partial g^*(\bar z)\), i.e., \(\mathrm{par}\,\partial g^*(\bar z):=\operatorname{span}(\partial g^*(\bar z)-u)\) for any \(u\in\partial g^*(\bar z)\).

Prove or disprove the following necessity statement. Suppose that one of the partially-fixed-parameter solution mappings is single-valued and locally Lipschitz around \((\bar A,\bar b,\bar\mu)\), for example:
- \(b\mapsto S(\bar A,b,\bar\mu)\) is single-valued and locally Lipschitz around \(\bar b\), or
- \((A,\mu)\mapsto S(A,\bar b,\mu)\) is single-valued and locally Lipschitz around \((\bar A,\bar\mu)\), or
- \(\mu\mapsto S(\bar A,\bar b,\mu)\) is single-valued and locally Lipschitz around \(\bar\mu\).
Under the standing assumptions above (including \(C^2\)-cone reducibility of \(g^*\) at \(\bar z\)), must the condition
\[
\ker \bar A\;\cap\; \mathrm{par}\,\partial g^*(\bar z)=\{0\}
\]
necessarily hold?
open_problem_quote: By fixing the parameter \(b=\bar b\), our condition (45) is also sufficient for the Lipschitz stability of the solution mappings considered after Theorem 3.8. Whether it is necessary is an open question.
